description: Resolving concurrent writes by comparing wall-clock timestamps drops a causally-later write whenever clocks are skewed.
body: Failure mode: conflict resolution keeps the write with the larger wall-clock timestamp, but NTP skew or a backward clock jump makes an older write appear newer, so a causally-later update is silently discarded. Detection signals: writes vanish without error under clock skew; a node with a fast clock always wins conflicts; vector-clock comparison shows the dropped write was concurrent or later. Do not use physical timestamps as cross-node version numbers. Safer fallback: track causality with vector clocks, surface true concurrent siblings for application-level merge (or a CRDT), and reserve timestamps for tie-breaking only with explicit uncertainty handling.
